heading: Indian corporations; appropriation for organizing
label: 5112 Indian corporations; appropriation for organizing
text: There is authorized to be appropriated, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such sums as may be necessary, but not to exceed $250,000 in any fiscal year, to be expended at the order of the Secretary of the Interior, in defraying the expenses of organizing Indian chartered corporations or other organizations created under this Act.
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in text, is , , popularly known as the Indian Reorganization Act, which is classified generally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act June 18, 1934, ch. 57648 Stat. 984section 5101 of this title
